owned.title: Mulholland Drive
owned.overview: Blonde Betty Elms has only just arrived in Hollywood to become a movie star when she meets an enigmatic brunette with amnesia. Meanwhile, as the two set off to solve the second woman's identity, filmmaker Adam Kesher runs into ominous trouble while casting his latest project.

owned.title: Eraserhead
owned.overview: First-time father Henry Spencer tries to survive his industrial environment, his angry girlfriend, and the unbearable screams of his newly born mutant child.
owned.local_id: Eraserhead (1977) Criterion (1080p BluRay x265 HEVC 10bit AAC 2.0 Tigole)

missing.title: Ant Head
missing.overview: Ants gather and consume a head-like shape in a static, abstract composition set to experimental music.

missing.title: Waiting for Mr. Lynch
missing.overview: In 2018, David Lynch was once again the focus of LEFFEST Lisboa Film Festival's programme, with the extensive 'Waiting for Mr. Lynch' retrospective. At the time, and because he was unable to attend the festival, Lynch offered a short film he made that week to be shown as a world premiere at the opening of the retrospective.

missing.title: WHAT DID JACK DO?
missing.overview: In a locked down train station, a homicide detective conducts an interview with a tormented monkey  who is suspected of murder.

missing.title: Fire (Pozar)
missing.overview: Lynch made the animation film Pozar (Fire) in 2015 after the Polish-American composer Marek Zebrowski created a musical composition as a tribute to him. The film was included in the 2018 retrospective 'Someone Is In My House'.

missing.title: Duran Duran: Unstaged
missing.overview: Duran Duran: Unstaged is a multimedia event that takes the audience on a cinematic journey with one of the most successful acts in the world during their performance at the Mayan Theater in Los Angeles.

missing.title: Twin Peaks: The Missing Pieces
missing.overview: A feature-length compilation of deleted and alternate takes from Twin Peaks: Fire Walk With Me, assembled by David Lynch to continue the story of the final week of Laura Palmer's life.

missing.title: Nine Inch Nails: Came Back Haunted
missing.overview: A promotional music video for Nine Inch Nails' 2013 single, directed by David Lynch.

missing.title: Idem Paris
missing.overview: A nearly wordless portrait of the renowned fine art printing studio in Paris, capturing the intricate process of lithography.

missing.title: Memory Film
missing.overview: Experimental short created for the 7th annual Memory Marathon at London's Serpentine Gallery, exploring the theme of memory through image and sound.

missing.title: 20 Little Films
missing.overview: Since 1995, the Viennale has invited renowned directors to create short, one-minute films as personal contributions to the festival. Ranging from home movies to political essays, musical sketches to abstract studies, these
form a unique anthology of cinematic moments. 20 Little Films collects a selection of these works, premiering together for the Viennale's 50th anniversary at the Locarno Film Festival.

missing.title: Blue Velvet: The Lost Footage
missing.overview: Fifty-three minutes of deleted scenes and alternate takes assembled by David Lynch.

missing.title: 42 One Dream Rush
missing.overview: An omnibus project bringing together acclaimed directors from around the world, each contributing a 42-second short inspired by a dream. Produced by 42 Below vodka, the collection blends surreal, poetic, and experimental visions into a mosaic of oneiric cinema.

missing.title: Hollyshorts Greeting
missing.overview: A fun short piece that Lynch presented as his acceptance for the Hollyshorts 2008 Visionary Award.

missing.title: Early Experiments
missing.overview: A 16mm film experiment conducted in 1967 and 1968 in Philadelphia which feature small parts of the Alphabet and the Grandmother.

missing.title: Twin Peaks Festival Greeting 2008
missing.overview: A surreal short film that Lynch made for the 2008 Twin Peaks Festival. It only screened once and is in black and white. In this film, Lynch is speaking backwards in what looks to be a cross between the floors of Eraserhead and the Twin Peaks Red Room. Sitting next to him is a plastic doll with the face of Laura Palmer.

missing.title: Dynamic:01: The Best of DavidLynch.com
missing.overview: Two hours of original content previously only available to paid up members of DavidLynch.com. This Best Of package includes 7 short films plus 3 intervalometer camera experiments. All shorts are written, directed, edited and scored by David Lynch with introductions from David before each item. Also includes 20 minutes of David Lynch answering an interesting variety of questions from members of his website. Contains The Darkened Room, Boat, Lamp, Out Yonder Neighbor Boy, Industrial Soundscape, The Bug Crawls, Inveralometer Experiments, Members Questions.

missing.title: Out Yonder - Chicken
missing.overview: Lost in thought, while staring at a silent bucket filled to the brim with water, David Lynch is shocked to discover that the ailing neighbour's chicken has gone missing.

missing.title: More Things That Happened
missing.overview: A collection of deleted scenes from David Lynch's 2006 surrealist horror INLAND EMPIRE.

missing.title: Ballerina
missing.overview: David Lynch turns this digital video of a ballerina into an impressionistic exercise.

missing.title: To Each His Own Cinema
missing.overview: Commissioned to mark the 60th anniversary of the Cannes Film Festival, 'To Each His Own Cinema' brought together 33 of the world's pre-eminent filmmakers to produce short pieces exploring the multifarious facets of cinema and their perspective on the state of their chosen artform in the early 21st century.

missing.title: Inland Empire
missing.overview: When actress Nikki Grace gets the lead role in a cursed film, her world becomes more and more surreal, blending realities and ideas of infidelity, reincarnation, and supernatural forces.

missing.title: Intervalometer Experiments
missing.overview: David Lynch experiments with time and photography in three different locations.

missing.title: Bug Crawls
missing.overview: In a solemn, haunted environment, a small bug crawls over the silhouette of a house.

missing.title: Out Yonder - Teeth
missing.overview: On a quiet winter morning, Austin, son of David Lynch, breaks his silence and decides to talk about his troubling functional incapacitation.

missing.title: Lamp
missing.overview: Short documentary of David Lynch building a lamp.

missing.title: Boat
missing.overview: A journey into night.

missing.title: The Disc of Sorrow Is Installed
missing.overview: The Disc of Sorrow gets installed.

missing.title: Coyote #1
missing.overview: A coyote somehow finds a way to a motel room.

missing.title: Dead Mouse with Ants
missing.overview: Features a dead mouse with ants on it.

missing.title: Pierre and Sonny Jim
missing.overview: Life can be hard on planet Earth, especially when you're running out of air.

missing.title: Rabbits
missing.overview: A story of a group of humanoid rabbits and their depressive, daily life. The plot includes Suzie ironing, Jane sitting on a couch, Jack walking in and out of the apartment, and the occasional solo singing number by Suzie or Jane. At one point the rabbits also make contact with their

missing.title: Darkened Room
missing.overview: Two young women find themselves in a dark room where there is a distinctly strange feeling.

missing.title: Industrial Soundscape
missing.overview: Music is produced by different objects in an industrial and desolate landscape.

missing.title: Steps
missing.overview: Time lapse of a flight of stairs. One of five segments from Intervalometer Experiments (2001-2003).

missing.title: BlueBob Egg
missing.overview: Before our very eyes, BlueBob gets arrested in broad daylight.

missing.title: The Pig Walks
missing.overview: No one knows what has been going on behind the closed doors of the city's macabre and noisome abattoir.

missing.title: Out Yonder - Neighbor Boy
missing.overview: Humorous short film in which David Lynch and his son Austin observe the follies of their neighbor.

missing.title: The Short Films of David Lynch
missing.overview: A collection of visionary director David Lynch's short films from the first 29 years of his career is accompanied by a special introduction to each film by the director himself.

missing.title: Factory Mask
missing.overview: Suddenly, in the cramped boiler room, a pressure-relief valve opens and discharges steam. Then, a certified technician approaches to perform a routine check. But, is he wearing his factory mask?

missing.title: Eraserhead Stories
missing.overview: David Lynch discusses the making of the film 'Eraserhead.'

missing.title: Sunset #1
missing.overview: Time lapse of a valley with light fading. One of five segments from Intervalometer Experiments (2001-2003).

missing.title: Head with Hammer
missing.overview: An aspect of life.

missing.title: One Morning All Over the World
missing.overview: An ad for an advertising company, consisting of 7-second sequence shots by 13 directors.

missing.title: The Straight Story
missing.overview: Retired farmer and widower Alvin Straight learns one day that his distant brother Lyle has suffered a stroke and may not recover. Alvin is determined to make things right with Lyle while he still can, but his brother lives in Wisconsin, while Alvin is stuck in Iowa with no car and no driver's license. He then has the idea of making the trip on his old lawnmower, thus beginning a picturesque and at times deeply spiritual odyssey.

missing.title: Lost Highway
missing.overview: A tormented jazz musician finds himself lost in an enigmatic story involving murder, surveillance, gangsters, doppelgängers, and an impossible transformation inside a prison cell.

missing.title: Premonitions Following an Evil Deed
missing.overview: A short film about the events following a murder.

missing.title: Lumière & Company
missing.overview: 40 international directors were asked to make a short film using the original Cinematographe invented by the Lumière Brothers, working under conditions similar to those of 1895. There were three rules: (1) The film could be no longer than 52 seconds, (2) no synchronized sound was permitted, and (3) no more than three takes.

missing.title: Michael Jackson: Dangerous - The Short Films
missing.overview: Michael Jackson's Dangerous album once again rocketed the global superstar to mega-platinum success! 'Dangerous - The Short Films' captures the visual and musical highlights of his most recent string of hit singles. As well as extensive behind the scenes profiles of the making of Michael's state of the art short films.

missing.title: Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me
missing.overview: In the questionable town of Deer Meadow, Washington, FBI Agent Desmond inexplicably disappears while hunting for the man who murdered a teen girl. The killer is never apprehended, and, after experiencing dark visions and supernatural encounters, Agent Dale Cooper chillingly predicts that the culprit will claim another life. Meanwhile, in the more cozy town of Twin Peaks, hedonistic beauty Laura Palmer hangs with lowlifes and seems destined for a grisly fate.

missing.title: The King of Ads
missing.overview: A collection of European T.V. commercials directed by a variety of well-known directors from across Europe and the U.S. Compiled and produced by Jean-Marie Boursicot.

missing.title: Industrial Symphony No. 1: The Dream of the Brokenhearted
missing.overview: After her boyfriend ends their relationship, the dreamself of a heartbroken woman floats through the air over an industrial wasteland singing ballads of love.

missing.title: Wild at Heart
missing.overview: Young lovers Sailor and Lula hit the road to start a new life together away from the wrath of Lula's deranged, disapproving mother, who has hired a team of hitmen to cut the lovers' surreal honeymoon short.

missing.title: Twin Peaks
missing.overview: An idiosyncratic FBI agent investigates the murder of a young woman in the even more idiosyncratic town of Twin Peaks. (This standalone version of the series pilot was produced for the European VHS market and has an alternate, closed ending.)

missing.title: The Cowboy and the Frenchman
missing.overview: A grizzled, hard-of-hearing cowboy, Slim, and his two friends, Dusty and Pete, capture a mysterious, well-dressed Frenchman.

missing.title: The French as Seen by.
missing.overview: In 1988, Figaro magazine asked a few famous directors to direct a series of short movies to celebrate the 10 years of the revue. The movies have been released for the French revolution bicentenary. Includes: Werner Herzog's Les Gaulois, David Lynch's The Cowboy and the Frenchman, Andrzej Wajda's Proust contre la déchéance, Luigi Comencini's Pèlerinage à Agen, Jean-Luc Godard's Le dernier mot.

missing.title: Blue Velvet
missing.overview: The discovery of a severed human ear found in a field leads a young man on an investigation related to a beautiful, mysterious nightclub singer and a group of psychopathic criminals who have kidnapped her child.

missing.title: Dune
missing.overview: In the year 10,191, the most precious substance in the universe is the spice Melange. The spice extends life. The spice expands consciousness. The spice is vital to space travel.  The spice exists on only one planet in the entire universe, the vast desert planet Arrakis, also known as Dune.  Its native inhabitants, the Fremen, have long held a prophecy that a man would come, a messiah who would lead them to true freedom.

missing.title: The Elephant Man
missing.overview: A Victorian surgeon rescues a heavily disfigured man being mistreated by his 'owner' as a side-show freak. Behind his monstrous façade, there is revealed a person of great intelligence and sensitivity. Based on the true story of Joseph Merrick (called John Merrick in the film), a severely deformed man in 19th century London.

missing.title: The Amputee
missing.overview: A double amputee attempts to write a letter while her nurse gets in the way.

missing.title: The Grandmother
missing.overview: To escape neglect and abuse from his parents, a young boy plants some strange seeds and they grow into a grandmother.

missing.title: The Alphabet
missing.overview: A woman's dark and absurdist nightmare vision comprising a continuous recitation of the alphabet and bizarre living representations of each letter.

missing.title: Sailing with Bushnell Keeler
missing.overview: In 1967, a young David Lynch grabbed his new Bolex 16mm camera, to film his friend and mentor Bushnell Keeler and brother Dave Keeler sailing on the Chesapeake Bay in Bush's King's Cruiser. This was David Lynch's very first film, which he prefers to call a 'home movie'. It depicts a man, a painter, who changed David's life forever pursuing the artist's life, which he continues to this day.

missing.title: Absurd Encounter with Fear
missing.overview: A man approaches a woman in a field.

missing.title: Six Men Getting Sick
missing.overview: Lynch's first film project consists of a looping animation of six people vomiting projected on to a special sculptured screen featuring twisted three-dimensional faces.

missing.title: Interview Project
missing.overview: Interview Project is a 20,000 mile road trip where 121 people where found at random and interviewed.
